1	package commit
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"strconv"
7		"strings"
8		"time"
9	
10		"github.com/expr-lang/expr"
11		metav1 "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1"
12	
13		kargoapi "github.com/akuity/kargo/api/v1alpha1"
14		"github.com/akuity/kargo/pkg/controller/git"
15		"github.com/akuity/kargo/pkg/logging"
16	)
17	
18	const branchPrefix = "refs/heads/"
19	
20	func init() {
21		defaultSelectorRegistry.MustRegister(
22			selectorRegistration{
23				Predicate: func(_ context.Context, sub kargoapi.GitSubscription) (bool, error) {
24					return sub.CommitSelectionStrategy == kargoapi.CommitSelectionStrategyNewestFromBranch ||
25						sub.CommitSelectionStrategy == "", nil
26				},
27				Value: newNewestFromBranchSelector,
28			},
29		)
30	}
31	
32	// newestFromBranchSelector implements the Selector interface for
33	// kargoapi.CommitSelectionStrategyNewestFromBranch.
34	type newestFromBranchSelector struct {
35		*baseSelector
36		branch string
37	
38		selectCommitsFn func(git.Repo) ([]git.CommitMetadata, error)
39		listCommitsFn   func(
40			repo git.Repo,
41			limit uint,
42			skip uint,
43		) ([]git.CommitMetadata, error)
44		getDiffPathsForCommitIDFn func(
45			repo git.Repo,
46			commitID string,
47		) ([]string, error)
48	}
49	
50	func newNewestFromBranchSelector(
51		sub kargoapi.GitSubscription,
52		creds *git.RepoCredentials,
53	) (Selector, error) {
54		base, err := newBaseSelector(sub, creds)
55		if err != nil {
56			return nil, fmt.Errorf("error building base selector: %w", err)
57		}
58		s := &newestFromBranchSelector{
59			baseSelector: base,
60			branch:       sub.Branch,
61		}
62		s.selectCommitsFn = s.selectCommits
63		s.listCommitsFn = s.listCommits
64		s.getDiffPathsForCommitIDFn = s.getDiffPathsForCommitID
65		return s, nil
66	}
67	
68	// MatchesRef implements Selector.
69	func (n *newestFromBranchSelector) MatchesRef(ref string) bool {
70		if !strings.HasPrefix(ref, branchPrefix) {
71			return false // Doesn't look like a branch ref
72		}
73		branch := strings.TrimPrefix(ref, "refs/heads/")
74		if n.branch == "" {
75			// Note: We do not require branch to be specified in the subscription.
76			// When it is not specified, the subscription is implicitly for the
77			// repository's default branch. We don't really know what the repository's
78			// default branch is, so we make two imperfect assumptions here:
79			//   1. The default branch is either "main" or "master".
80			//   2. The repository does not have BOTH a main and master branch.
81			return branch == "main" || branch == "master"
82		}
83		return n.branch == branch
84	}
85	
86	// Select implements the Selector interface.
87	func (n *newestFromBranchSelector) Select(ctx context.Context) (
88		[]kargoapi.DiscoveredCommit,
89		error,
90	) {
91		loggerCtx := append(
92			n.getLoggerContext(),
93			"selectionStrategy", kargoapi.CommitSelectionStrategyNewestFromBranch,
94			"branch", n.branch,
95		)
96		logger := logging.LoggerFromContext(ctx).WithValues(loggerCtx...)
97		ctx = logging.ContextWithLogger(ctx, logger)
98	
99		logger.Debug("cloning repository")
100		repo, err := n.gitCloneFn(
101			n.repoURL,
102			&git.ClientOptions{
103				Credentials:           n.creds,
104				InsecureSkipTLSVerify: n.insecureSkipTLSVerify,
105			},
106			&git.CloneOptions{
107				Branch:       n.branch,
108				SingleBranch: true,
109				Filter:       git.FilterBlobless,
110			},
111		)
112		if err != nil {
113			return nil, fmt.Errorf("error cloning git repo %q: %w", n.repoURL, err)
114		}
115		defer func() {
116			_ = repo.Close()
117		}()
118	
119		commits, err := n.selectCommitsFn(repo)
120		if err != nil {
121			return nil,
122				fmt.Errorf("error selecting relevant commits from branch: %w", err)
123		}
124	
125		return n.commitsToAPICommits(ctx, commits), nil
126	}
127	
128	func (n *newestFromBranchSelector) selectCommits(
129		repo git.Repo,
130	) ([]git.CommitMetadata, error) {
131		selectedCommits := make([]git.CommitMetadata, 0, n.discoveryLimit)
132		for skip, batch := uint(0), uint(n.discoveryLimit); ; skip, batch = skip+batch, min(batch*2, 1000) { // nolint: gosec
133			commits, err := n.listCommitsFn(repo, batch, skip) // nolint: gosec
134			if err != nil {
135				return nil,
136					fmt.Errorf("error listing commits from git repo %q: %w", n.repoURL, err)
137			}
138	
139			if len(commits) == 0 {
140				break
141			}
142	
143			// If no filters are specified, return the first commits up to the limit.
144			if n.includePaths == nil && n.excludePaths == nil && n.filterExpression == nil {
145				return trimSlice(commits, n.discoveryLimit), nil
146			}
147	
148			for _, commit := range commits {
149				// Filter commits based on expressions.
150				include, err := n.evaluateCommitExpression(commit)
151				if err != nil {
152					return nil, fmt.Errorf("error evaluating filter expression: %w", err)
153				}
154				if !include {
155					continue
156				}
157	
158				// If include or exclude path selectors are specified, filter the commits.
159				if n.includePaths != nil || n.excludePaths != nil {
160					diffPaths, err := n.getDiffPathsForCommitIDFn(repo, commit.ID)
161					if err != nil {
162						return nil, fmt.Errorf(
163							"error getting diff paths for commit %q in git repo %q: %w",
164							commit.ID,
165							n.repoURL,
166							err,
167						)
168					}
169					if !n.MatchesPaths(diffPaths) {
170						continue
171					}
172				}
173	
174				// If we reach this point, the commit got past all the filters.
175				selectedCommits = append(selectedCommits, commit)
176	
177				if len(selectedCommits) >= n.discoveryLimit {
178					return trimSlice(selectedCommits, n.discoveryLimit), nil
179				}
180			}
181		}
182	
183		return trimSlice(selectedCommits, n.discoveryLimit), nil
184	}
185	
186	func (n *newestFromBranchSelector) listCommits(
187		repo git.Repo,
188		limit uint,
189		skip uint,
190	) ([]git.CommitMetadata, error) {
191		return repo.ListCommits(limit, skip) // nolint: gosec
192	}
193	
194	func (n *newestFromBranchSelector) getDiffPathsForCommitID(
195		repo git.Repo,
196		commitID string,
197	) ([]string, error) {
198		return repo.GetDiffPathsForCommitID(commitID)
199	}
200	
201	// evaluateCommitExpression evaluates the given commit expression against
202	// the given commit metadata. The commit metadata is passed as the environment
203	// for the expression evaluation. It returns true if the expression evaluates to
204	// true, and false otherwise. If the expression is not a boolean, it is
205	// converted to a boolean using strconv.ParseBool. If the conversion fails,
206	// an error is returned.
207	func (n *newestFromBranchSelector) evaluateCommitExpression(
208		commit git.CommitMetadata,
209	) (bool, error) {
210		if n.filterExpression == nil {
211			return true, nil
212		}
213	
214		env := map[string]any{
215			"id":         commit.ID,
216			"commitDate": commit.CommitDate,
217			"author":     commit.Author,
218			"committer":  commit.Committer,
219			"subject":    commit.Subject,
220		}
221	
222		result, err := expr.Run(n.filterExpression, env)
223		if err != nil {
224			return false, err
225		}
226	
227		switch result := result.(type) {
228		case bool:
229			return result, nil
230		default:
231			parsedBool, err := strconv.ParseBool(fmt.Sprintf("%v", result))
232			if err != nil {
233				return false, err
234			}
235			return parsedBool, nil
236		}
237	}
238	
239	func (n *newestFromBranchSelector) commitsToAPICommits(
240		ctx context.Context,
241		commits []git.CommitMetadata,
242	) []kargoapi.DiscoveredCommit {
243		logger := logging.LoggerFromContext(ctx)
244		apiCommits := make([]kargoapi.DiscoveredCommit, 0, len(commits))
245		for _, meta := range commits {
246			apiCommits = append(apiCommits, kargoapi.DiscoveredCommit{
247				ID:     meta.ID,
248				Branch: n.branch,
249				// A decent subject length for a commit message is 50 characters
250				// (based on the 50/72 rule). We are nice people, and allow a
251				// bit more. But not an excessive amount, to minimize the risk of
252				// exceeding the maximum size of the object in the API server.
253				Subject:     shortenString(meta.Subject, 80),
254				Author:      meta.Author,
255				Committer:   meta.Committer,
256				CreatorDate: &metav1.Time{Time: meta.CommitDate},
257			})
258			logger.Trace(
259				"discovered commit from branch",
260				"commit", meta.ID,
261				"creatorDate", meta.CommitDate.Format(time.RFC3339),
262			)
263		}
264		logger.Debug("discovered commits", "count", len(commits))
265		return apiCommits
266	}

package git

import (
	"fmt"
	"net/url"
	"os"
	"testing"

	"github.com/google/uuid"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestRepo(t *testing.T) {
	testServer, testRepoURL, testRepoCreds := setupRemoteRepo(t)
	defer testServer.Close()

	rep, err := Clone(
		testRepoURL,
		&ClientOptions{
			Credentials: &testRepoCreds,
		},
		nil,
	)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.NotNil(t, rep)
	defer rep.Close()
	r, ok := rep.(*repo)
	require.True(t, ok)

	t.Run("can clone", func(t *testing.T) {
		require.Equal(t, testRepoURL, r.originalURL)
		var accessURL *url.URL
		accessURL, err = url.Parse(r.accessURL)
		require.NoError(t, err)
		accessURL.User = nil
		require.Equal(t, testRepoURL, accessURL.String())
		require.NotEmpty(t, r.homeDir)
		var fi os.FileInfo
		fi, err = os.Stat(r.homeDir)

	require.NoError(t, err)

		require.NoError(t, err)

		err = os.WriteFile(fmt.Sprintf("%s/local.txt", repo.Dir()), []byte("local"), 0o600)
		require.NoError(t, err)
		err = repo.AddAllAndCommit("local commit", nil)
		require.NoError(t, err)

		// Enable signing so that RebaseOrMerge would fall back to merge, but
		// AlwaysRebase should still rebase.
		wt := internalWorkTree(t, repo)
		enableFakeCommitSigning(t, wt, true)

		err = wt.integrateBeforePush(
			"ahead", nil, PushIntegrationPolicyAlwaysRebase,
		)
		require.NoError(t, err)

		// A rebase produces no merge commits:
		// initial + remote + local = 3.
		commits, err := repo.ListCommits(0, 0)
		require.NoError(t, err)
		require.Len(t, commits, 3)
	})

	t.Run("RebaseOrMerge: rebases when safe", func(t *testing.T) {
		repo, err := Clone(testRepoURL, &ClientOptions{Credentials: &testRepoCreds}, nil)
		require.NoError(t, err)

		err = os.WriteFile(fmt.Sprintf("%s/local.txt", repo.Dir()), []byte("local"), 0o600)
		require.NoError(t, err)

		require.NoError(t, err)

		err = internalWorkTree(t, repo).integrateBeforePush(
			"ahead", nil, PushIntegrationPolicyRebaseOrMerge,

		require.NoError(t, err)

		// All local commits are unsigned and signing is not configured, so
		// rebase is safe. No merge commits: initial + remote + local = 3.
		commits, err := repo.ListCommits(0, 0)

		require.Len(t, commits, 3)

		require.NoError(t, err)
		err = repo.AddAllAndCommit("local commit", nil)
		require.NoError(t, err)

		// Enable signing so that rebasing unsigned commits would be unsafe.
		wt := internalWorkTree(t, repo)
		enableFakeCommitSigning(t, wt, true)

		err = wt.integrateBeforePush(
			"ahead", nil, PushIntegrationPolicyRebaseOrMerge,
		)
		require.NoError(t, err)

		// Should have fallen back to merge.
		msg, err := repo.CommitMessage("HEAD")
		require.NoError(t, err)
		require.Contains(t, msg, "Merge")
	})

	t.Run("RebaseOrFail: rebases when safe", func(t *testing.T) {
		repo, err := Clone(testRepoURL, &ClientOptions{Credentials: &testRepoCreds}, nil)
		require.NoError(t, err)

		err = os.WriteFile(fmt.Sprintf("%s/local.txt", repo.Dir()), []byte("local"), 0o600)
		require.NoError(t, err)

		require.NoError(t, err)

		err = internalWorkTree(t, repo).integrateBeforePush(

18	// setupRemoteRepo creates a gitkit-backed remote repository accessible via an
19	// http server, returning the server, repo URL, and credentials, if applicable.
20	// The caller must close the server when done with it.
21	func setupRemoteRepo(
22		t *testing.T,
23		initFns ...func(t *testing.T, repo WorkTree),
24	) (*httptest.Server, string, RepoCredentials) {
25		t.Helper()
26	
27		creds := RepoCredentials{
28			Username: "test-user",
29			Password: "test-pass",
30		}
31	
32		var useAuth bool
33		if s := os.Getenv("TEST_GIT_CLIENT_WITH_AUTH"); s != "" {
34			useAuth = types.MustParseBool(s)
35		}
36		service := gitkit.New(gitkit.Config{
37			Dir:        t.TempDir(),
38			AutoCreate: true,
39			Auth:       useAuth,
40		})
41		require.NoError(t, service.Setup())
42		service.AuthFunc = func(
43			c gitkit.Credential, _ *gitkit.Request,
44		) (bool, error) {
45			return c.Username == creds.Username &&
46				c.Password == creds.Password, nil
47		}
48		server := httptest.NewServer(service)
49		// server must be closed by the caller
50	
51		testRepoURL := fmt.Sprintf("%s/test.git", server.URL)
52	
53		repo, err := Clone(testRepoURL, &ClientOptions{Credentials: &creds}, nil)
54		require.NoError(t, err)
55		require.NotNil(t, repo)
56		defer repo.Close()
57	
58		for _, initFn := range initFns {
59			initFn(t, repo)
60		}
61	
62		return server, testRepoURL, creds
63	}
64	
65	func initialMainCommit(t *testing.T, rep WorkTree) {
66		err := os.WriteFile(

73		require.NoError(t, err)
74		err = rep.Push(nil)
75		require.NoError(t, err)
76	}

79		err := os.WriteFile(

88		require.NoError(t, err)
89	}

func TestRepo(t *testing.T) {

	rep, err := Clone(
		testRepoURL,
		&ClientOptions{
			Credentials: &testRepoCreds,
		},
		nil,
	)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.NotNil(t, rep)
	defer rep.Close()
	r, ok := rep.(*repo)
	require.True(t, ok)

		require.NotEmpty(t, r.homeDir)
		var fi os.FileInfo
		fi, err = os.Stat(r.homeDir)
		require.NoError(t, err)
		require.True(t, fi.IsDir())
		require.NotEmpty(t, r.dir)
		fi, err = os.Stat(r.dir)
		require.NoError(t, err)
		require.True(t, fi.IsDir())
	})

	t.Run("can get the repo url", func(t *testing.T) {
		require.Equal(t, r.originalURL, r.URL())

		require.Equal(t, r.homeDir, r.HomeDir())
	})

		require.Equal(t, r.dir, r.Dir())
	})

	require.NoError(t, err)

	t.Run("can check for diffs -- positive result", func(t *testing.T) {
		var hasDiffs bool
		hasDiffs, err = rep.HasDiffs()
		require.NoError(t, err)
		require.True(t, hasDiffs)
	})

with a body
`, uuid.NewString())
	err = rep.AddAllAndCommit(testCommitMessage, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	t.Run("can commit", func(t *testing.T) {
		require.NoError(t, err)
	})

	lastCommitID, err := rep.LastCommitID()
	require.NoError(t, err)

	t.Run("can get last commit id", func(t *testing.T) {
		require.NoError(t, err)
		require.NotEmpty(t, lastCommitID)
	})

	t.Run("can get commit message by id", func(t *testing.T) {
		var msg string
		msg, err = rep.CommitMessage(lastCommitID)
		require.NoError(t, err)
		require.Equal(t, testCommitMessage, msg)
	})

	t.Run("can get diff paths", func(t *testing.T) {
		var paths []string
		paths, err = rep.GetDiffPathsForCommitID(lastCommitID)
		require.NoError(t, err)
		require.Len(t, paths, 1)
	})

	t.Run("can check if remote branch exists -- negative result", func(t *testing.T) {
		var exists bool
		exists, err = rep.RemoteBranchExists("main") // The remote repo is empty!
		require.NoError(t, err)
		require.False(t, exists)
	})

	err = rep.Push(nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	t.Run("can push", func(t *testing.T) {
		require.NoError(t, err)
	})

	t.Run("can check if remote branch exists -- positive result", func(t *testing.T) {
		var exists bool
		exists, err = rep.RemoteBranchExists("main")
		require.NoError(t, err)
		require.True(t, exists)
	})

	testBranch := fmt.Sprintf("test-branch-%s", uuid.NewString())
	err = rep.CreateChildBranch(testBranch)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	t.Run("can create a child branch", func(t *testing.T) {
		require.NoError(t, err)
	})

	err = os.WriteFile(fmt.Sprintf("%s/%s", rep.Dir(), "test.txt"), []byte("bar"), 0600)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	t.Run("can hard reset", func(t *testing.T) {
		var hasDiffs bool
		hasDiffs, err = rep.HasDiffs()
		require.NoError(t, err)
		require.True(t, hasDiffs)
		err = rep.ResetHard()
		require.NoError(t, err)
		hasDiffs, err = rep.HasDiffs()
		require.NoError(t, err)
		require.False(t, hasDiffs)
	})

	t.Run("can create an orphaned branch", func(t *testing.T) {
		testBranch := fmt.Sprintf("test-branch-%s", uuid.NewString())
		err = rep.CreateOrphanedBranch(testBranch)
		require.NoError(t, err)
	})

	t.Run("can load an existing repo", func(t *testing.T) {
		existingRepo, err := LoadRepo(
			rep.Dir(),
			&LoadRepoOptions{
				Credentials: &testRepoCreds,
			},
		)
		require.NoError(t, err)
		require.Equal(t, rep, existingRepo)
	})

	t.Run("can close repo", func(t *testing.T) {
		require.NoError(t, rep.Close())
		_, err := os.Stat(r.HomeDir())
		require.Error(t, err)
		require.True(t, os.IsNotExist(err))
	})

}

content.text:
Implemented the fix for path filtering on merge commits.

What changed:
- Updated `pkg/controller/git/work_tree.go`
- `GetDiffPathsForCommitID()` now runs:

```sh
git show --pretty= --name-only --first-parent <commitID>
```

instead of:

```sh
git show --pretty= --name-only <commitID>
```

This makes merge commits report only the paths introduced relative to the first parent, avoiding both:
